DAMAGE POINTS INITIALLY WERE IN THREE EAST/WEST ORIENTED LINES ALONG BOROWICK CIRCLE. THE LINES OF DAMAGE AS WELL AS THE DIRECTION THE TREES FELL WOULD INDICATE STRAIGHT-LINED WINDS. IT APPEARS MUCH OF THE WIND MUST HAVE STAYED JUST OFF THE SURFACE, THUS AFFECTING MAINLY THE TALLER TREES.
MORE DAMAGE IN THE GRAND DELL SUBDIVISION, WHERE SEVERAL TREES WERE FELLED, MOSTLY TOWARD THE EAST, AND SOME PROPERTIES WERE DAMAGED.

A VERY NARROW INTENSE DAMAGE PATH (25 YARDS) OF ROTATING 90-95 MPH WINDS EMBEDDED WITHIN A 3-MILE WIDE AREA OF STRAIGHT-LINED WINDS. THE TORNADO LEFT A DISTINCT PATH IN THE VEGETATION AND DESTROYED PART OF A LARGE METAL BARN NEAR THE ROSETTA GENERAL STORE. IT ALSO CAUSED THE WALLS OF 2 ADJACENT OUTBUILDINGS AND A GARAGE TO BUCKLE OUT.

TORNADO HAD AN 8.6 MILE PATH LENGTH AND GOT AS WIDE AS 300 YARDS. DAMAGE OCCURRED TO MULTIPLE TREES AND PROPERTIES. TORNADO ENDED 2 MILES WSW OF OLATON.
